claim: AI startups captured 50% of global funding in 2025, up dramatically from historical patterns, indicating market restructuring toward AI
actionable: Monitor AI startup funding trends as an indicator of market direction; recognize that half of all venture capital now flows to AI companies

claim: DeepSeek R1 caused $600 billion in Nvidia market cap loss in single day, largest one-day loss in US stock history
actionable: Track geopolitical AI competition; recognize that open-source breakthroughs from non-US labs can cause immediate market disruptions

claim: Stargate project is $500B investment over 4 years to build AI infrastructure for OpenAI, indicating massive capital allocation required for frontier AI
actionable: Understand that competing in frontier AI requires multi-hundred-billion-dollar infrastructure investments; this raises barriers for new entrants

claim: AI coding assistants are the primary application success story in 2025, with Cursor reaching $500M ARR and Claude Code reaching $1B revenue
actionable: Focus resources on AI-assisted coding as the most market-validated AI application; this vertical shows strongest commercial traction

claim: Model Context Protocol (MCP) adoption by OpenAI, Anthropic, and major labs indicates it is becoming standard for AI tool integration
actionable: Build MCP-compatible tools and integrations now to ensure compatibility with multiple LLM providers; avoid lock-in to single vendor APIs

claim: Microsoft 365 Copilot reached 90% adoption among Fortune 500 companies by November 2025, fastest enterprise AI adoption ever
actionable: Integrate with Microsoft 365 for enterprise reach; recognize that Office productivity is primary enterprise AI entry point

claim: Claude Opus 4.5 is positioned as dominant AI coding model according to benchmarks and market adoption metrics
actionable: Use Claude Opus 4.5 as baseline model for coding tasks; benchmark against it to validate other model choices

claim: Open-source LLMs (Qwen 3, GLM 4.7, DeepSeek 3.2) are approaching closed-source model capability while offering cheaper inference
actionable: Evaluate open-source models as cost-effective alternatives for inference; run benchmarks to compare quality vs. cost tradeoff
